Goal is to select vendor by EOY and start implementing in 2027. S2C with requisitions and intake in scope. Sent RFP template, should receive RFP in fall 5/1 BCE: Tammy reached out asking for pricing. Sent over QIT form 4/14 BCE: RFI Submitted 4/3 BCE: Due next Friday 3/27 BCE: Intro occurred early march. RFI came in this week. Due in 2 weeks
hard.ais_why:
• Intake is in scope as part of S2C with requisitions, but it is framed as scope rather than buyer-voice AI excitement.
• Description is empty and there are no Avoma calls. Engagement is RFI/RFP-driven, with the RFP expected back in the fall.
• Qualified, $120K. Vendor selection by EOY 2026 and 2027 implementation. No urgency or AI mandate signaled.

north_star_verdict.headline: Live but quiet multi-vendor S2C-plus-risk evaluation led by two procurement evaluators; buyer has been silent since mid-May and the 15 Dec close leaves no room for RFP, commercials and contracting.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 02 Mar 2026, in Formal Evaluation since 30 May 2026. Last genuine buyer touch was Tammy Clark's 16 May note asking for the summit recording; the 21 May activity was an out-of-office auto-reply, and Bailey's 15 Jun check-in returned 'no updates.' So the buyer has been quiet roughly 50 days as of 06 Jul. Buyer-stated plan: RFP returns in the fall, vendor selected by end of 2026, implementation 2027. Close date 15 Dec 2026 (162 days out) sits at the very end of the selection window and leaves no buffer for RFP evaluation, shortlist, commercials, economic-buyer sign-off, MSA/SOW, legal or InfoSec, conservatively 4-6 months from RFP issuance. Current pace is buyer-controlled and normal for early enterprise, but the forecast date will likely slip into Q1 2027.
evidence: Buyer stated goal on the 02 Mar intro call: select a vendor by end of 2026, implementation late 2026 or 2027 (Tammy Clark and Matt Palka)
RFI submitted 14 Apr 2026 per Next_Step__c; RFP expected back in the fall per the 08 May update
Pricing thread 01-08 May: Tammy asked for line-item pricing, Bailey sent the QIT form and a module-level pricing table on 08 May
Horizon 2026 invite sent to Tammy and Matt on 20 May; Tammy's 16 May reply asking for the summit recording is the last genuine buyer touch
Bailey's 15 Jun check-in returned 'No updates. Project kicks off in the fall' per Next_Step__c
No buyer-initiated contact in roughly 50 days; engagement has thinned though the buyer's fall-RFP timeline is intact
Close date 15 Dec sits at the end of the selection window with no buffer for negotiation, contracting, legal or InfoSec

metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated the business problem clearly but never quantified it. Tammy Clark stated the consolidation goal on the 02 Mar intro call: 'Ideally, we'll find suppliers that have both, and we won't need to do two giant RFPs and implement two tools,' and described the core spend-visibility pain: 'We wanna have all of our data in one place... we don't have all that data in one place. We're not seeing the leakage, and we're not seeing our total spend with the supplier.' Today spend sits in three silos (SAP S4 for PO, a separate T&E/credit-card expense system, and manual tracking). No savings target, cycle-time reduction, headcount efficiency or ROI figure has been stated. The move is to run a value-discovery session before the fall RFP to quantify spend leakage, RFX cycle-time savings and contract-compliance lift.

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is named in any Salesforce field, contact role or call. Both mapped contacts, Tammy Clark (Category Manager) and Matt Palka (Manager, Indirect Procurement), are category-level procurement, not budget owners. The evidence does confirm funded budget exists: Tammy said on 02 Mar, 'We have budget. Our fiscal year starts April one. So we do have some budget for a tool,' and constrained it: 'We don't have the budget for two platforms.' That reveals a single-platform budget envelope but not who signs for it. This is an early-stage risk: mapping the budget owner (likely a procurement or finance leader above Tammy) and getting into that room before the fall RFP is a ranked move.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's criteria surfaced concretely on the 02 Mar call even though no formal scoring rubric is documented. First, a single unified platform covering both Source-to-Contract and Supplier Risk Management to avoid running two RFPs. Second, strength specifically in supplier risk: Tammy pressed 'right up front, we wanna try to get a good idea of your strength in the supplier risk management area,' and probed the data model, 'What data do you actually provide? Because we're not looking for an aggregator that's going to require us to have a dozen subscriptions,' naming Experian as their financial-risk source. Third, deep bi-directional SAP integration (vendor master lives in SAP; requisition-to-PO and invoicing stay in SAP). Fourth, contract repository with renewal reminders and RFX automation to replace manual and homegrown tools. These are confirmed asks; formal weighting will come with the fall RFP.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the activity sequence, the buyer is running a structured, procurement-led evaluation: intro/discovery on 02 Mar, an RFI issued mid-March and submitted by Zycus 14 Apr, a pricing/QIT round through early May, and a full RFP the buyer will issue in the fall with a stated goal to select a vendor by end of 2026 and implement in 2027. Tammy and Matt jointly run the evaluation ('Matt and I have been tasked with looking at a source-to-pay platform'). No senior approver or steering stakeholder has yet joined a call, and the approval chain above the two evaluators is unknown. The forcing function is the buyer's own fall-RFP-then-year-end-selection cadence, not an external event. Mapping who signs off and how the RFP will be scored is the process gap to close.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet in play, which is normal at Formal Evaluation. The only paper signal is Tammy's early intent to exchange an NDA: on 02 Mar she said 'I'm gonna do is send over an NDA... can you send me your company's legal name and headquarters address,' and the 23 Mar email trail shows it routed to 'Mike through DocuSign.' No MSA, Order Form, SOW, legal-review path, procurement channel or InfoSec process has been discussed. As a US healthcare buyer, expect a security/data-handling review and, given SAP integration, an ERP-integration scope step later. What would create visibility is the fall RFP itself, which typically names the contracting and security requirements.
sources: Task 23 Mar 2026 (Sysmex - Zycus Follow Up, NDA to Mike via DocuSign)

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer articulated concrete operational pains on 02 Mar. Manual sourcing: Tammy said, 'We don't have a tool for our RFXs. We do all of that manually.' A limited homegrown contract tool: Matt described it as 'a homegrown tool for document repository, for intake forms... the system basically sends you reminders every 36 to 90 days,' and said the 'main reason for a whole new tool is just to have the repository.' Fragmented spend and no leakage visibility: Tammy said suppliers may be paid both on a PO and by credit card, 'so we don't have all that data in one place. We're not seeing the leakage, and we're not seeing our total spend with the supplier.' Duplicate vendor records in SAP were also raised. Pain is confirmed and owned by Tammy and Matt in indirect procurement.

champion.narrative: Tammy Clark (Category Manager, Travel & Expense, Fleet, Professional Services; PRIMARY contact) is a developing, not confirmed, champion. What has developed: she owns the evaluation, reached out unprompted for pricing on 01 May, engaged the whole RFI/pricing thread, and requested the RFP template, Gartner report and an NDA, all signs of genuine intent. What is missing: no evidence of internal advocacy, no competitive intel shared, no introduction to a budget owner, finance or IT, and no pushback on rivals for Zycus. Matt Palka (Manager, Indirect Procurement) is a supportive co-evaluator who clarified the SAP integration boundary. The relationship is cooperative but transactional and procurement-led, and it has cooled: no buyer-initiated contact since Tammy's 16 May note. Securing Tammy as a true champion and multi-threading to power is the priority move.
sources: OpportunityContactRole (Tammy Clark PRIMARY Evaluator, Matt Palka Evaluator)

competition.narrative: No competitor has been named in any source. Competitors__c is empty, no rival was mentioned on either call, and no vendor names appear in the activity trail. Tammy did confirm it is a competitive process, 'we've talked to a number of suppliers,' on 02 Mar, and 'right now, we're just trying to get the lay of the land,' so other vendors have also responded to the RFI. The field is expected to narrow through the fall RFP. Zycus's wedge is the unified single-platform S2C plus embedded Supplier Risk (directly answering Tammy's stated goal to avoid two RFPs and two tools), deep bi-directional SAP integration, verified-source risk monitoring that does not force many subscriptions, and the Merlin AI narrative. The competitive read is a genuine knowledge gap; surfacing the shortlist by asking Tammy directly is a ranked move.

deal_movement.summary: The deal progressed on paper (Qualified to Formal Evaluation on 30 May, amount raised from 120k to 200k on 20 May after the pricing round) but buyer engagement has thinned: the last genuine buyer touch was Tammy's 16 May note, and Bailey's 15 Jun check-in returned 'no updates, project kicks off in the fall.'

items.change: Stage advanced Qualified to Formal Evaluation

competitive_position.summary: No competitor is named in any source to date. Tammy Clark confirmed a multi-vendor process on 02 Mar ('we've talked to a number of suppliers') but named none, and the RFP that will formalize the shortlist is not due until the fall, so the field is a real knowledge gap rather than a sole-source win. There is no evidence any rival is preferred or ahead; the immediate task is to surface the shortlist so Zycus can position its unified S2C-plus-Risk wedge and SAP-integration depth against a known field before the RFP response.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus maps well to the buyer's stated unified-platform and RFX-automation needs, but two capability areas the buyer probed hard (risk data sources and pulling T&E/credit-card data into spend analysis) are only partially proven and one commitment to verify was left open.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter most now: break out of single-threaded procurement contact to reach the budget owner, surface the unnamed competitive field before the fall RFP, and convert the moderate AI signal into a quantified value story the buyer will score.
flags: Single-threaded to two category-level procurement evaluators (Tammy, Matt) with no EB, finance or IT access; map the budget owner before the fall RFP.
Competitive field is entirely unnamed in an active multi-vendor RFP; ask Tammy directly who else is in the running to position the unified S2C-plus-Risk wedge.
Merlin/AI story was presented but did not resonate (AIS Moderate); lead the RFP response with a quantified spend-leakage and RFX-automation value case, not a feature tour.

items.name: Tammy Clark
items.title: Category Manager - Travel & Expense, Fleet, Professional Services
items.risk: Developing champion, not yet advocating internally; single-threaded and no access to power. Engagement thinning.
items.sentiment: Engaged and cooperative, owns the evaluation, requested pricing, RFP template and NDA; has cooled since her 16 May note with no buyer-initiated contact since.

items.name: Matt Palka, MBA, CSP

champion_strength.summary: Tammy Clark owns the evaluation and shows genuine intent (unprompted pricing request 01 May, asked for the RFP template, Gartner report and NDA), but she is developing, not confirmed: no internal advocacy, no competitive intel shared, no intro to a budget owner or IT/finance, and engagement has cooled with no buyer-initiated contact since her 16 May note. Matt Palka backs her as a co-evaluator. The relationship is procurement-led and transactional, and single-threaded below power.
champion_strength.source: OpportunityContactRole; intro call 02 Mar 2026; Next_Step__c (pricing request 01 May); Task 16 May 2026

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led with a broad AI/Merlin story on 02 Mar (autonomous negotiation agents, AI contract review and risk scoring, Teams-embedded Merlin assist), but the buyer engaged on integration and risk data sources, not AI, and never voiced an AI mandate. The AI value is currently feature-told, not tied to Sysmex's spend-leakage and manual-RFX pain, so it is under-positioned for the RFP round.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 5.0 (Moderate). The field read is corroborated by the calls: AI capability was presented but the buyer showed no AI urgency and framed intake/AI as scope, not excitement. Genuine but not a hungry AI buyer; the Merlin narrative has room to be quantified.

customer_commitment.evidence: Still exploratory: 'trying to get the lay of the land'; no EB, no Zycus-specific decision step; only template/report/NDA requested.

scope_change.detail: Amount rose from 120k to 200k on 20 May 2026 after the pricing discussion; functional scope is scoped to Source-to-Contract plus Supplier Risk with a requisition/intake catalog front-end, with requisition-to-PO and invoicing deliberately staying in SAP per Matt Palka on 02 Mar.

customer_preference.evidence: Neutral, research-led posture: Tammy said 'we're just trying to get the lay of the land' (02 Mar) and asked only for a template, Gartner report and NDA; no favorable comparison versus any rival and no Zycus-specific decision step.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain only: manual RFX, limited homegrown contract tool, and fragmented spend ('we're not seeing the leakage, and we're not seeing our total spend with the supplier', 02 Mar). No savings, cycle-time or ROI figure quantified.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 7 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

commentary.win_position: Win 29 is carried by momentum 58 vs stage-expected 52; champion strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 58 (moving forward): Next Step carries 7 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 15 is anchored on the stage win (29), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 58, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
